$comment: test: plan which knowledge libraries to retrieve, generate a Go implementation AND a test grounded on them, then verify BEHAVIOR with go vet + go test (the go_test oracle), repairing ONCE if vet/tests fail. plan -> generate -> check -> done | fix -> recheck -> done | fail. Bounded, unrolled repair (no cycle) so it lints clean and terminates. plan emits one search query per library (stdlib/patterns/idioms); an empty query is skipped, so only the relevant libraries are consulted (cpp-style plan-routing). The chain is named `test` (not `go_test`) because the flat namespace forbids a chain and a tool sharing a name; the oracle tool is `go_test`.
summary: Generate a Go implementation plus a test for a task, verify it with `go vet` + `go test` (behavior, not just compile), and repair once if it fails.
